Title: Development of rubber-enriched dandelion varieties by metabolic engineering of the inulin pathway

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Tb1.2; Tb4.2; Tk1.7; Tk2.8; Tk8.1; controls Tb-WT; Tk3.1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter used to overexpress Tk1-FEH in the applied species
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; endogenous REF transcript increased in some Tk1-FEH overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots of 18-week-old transgenic lines; significantly increased in Tb1.2, Tk2.8 and Tk8.1
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Rubber elongation factor; rubber particle-associated/stabilizing protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial raw material for numerous products
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher endogenous REF expression correlated with increased natural rubber accumulation in Tk1-FEH overexpression backgrounds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF transcript levels rose in high-Tk1-FEH lines with elevated rubber content. 2. REF is part of the rubber-particle machinery associated with enhanced poly(cis-1,4-isoprene) accumulation. 3. REF itself was not directly engineered in this study but served as a core metabolism-relevant readout linked to rubber increase.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR correlation with metabolite profiling by 1H-NMR
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent fitness penalty in Tk1-FEH overexpression lines showing higher REF-associated rubber accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful companion marker with Tk1-FEH/CPT/RTA for selecting high-rubber genotypes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: REF was not the primary transgene; included because the Results section links its endogenous expression to the increased rubber phenotype.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Tb1.2; Tb4.2; Tk1.7; Tk2.8; Tk8.1; controls Tb-WT; Tk3.1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter used to overexpress Tk1-FEH in the applied species
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; endogenous RTA transcript increased in some Tk1-FEH overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots of 18-week-old transgenic lines; significantly increased in Tb1.2, Tk2.8 and Tk8.1
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Rubber transferase activator / rubber biosynthesis complex component
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial raw material for numerous products
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher endogenous RTA expression correlated with increased natural rubber accumulation in Tk1-FEH overexpression backgrounds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RTA transcript abundance correlated with elevated natural rubber levels in Tk1-FEH-overexpressing plants. 2. RTA is part of the rubber transferase complex associated with enhanced poly(cis-1,4-isoprene) synthesis. 3. RTA was monitored as a mechanistic link between sugar release and rubber biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR correlation with metabolite profiling by 1H-NMR
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent fitness penalty in lines with elevated RTA-associated rubber increase
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-selection marker or stacking target together with FEH/CPT/REF for rubber enhancement
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RTA was not directly manipulated; it responded endogenously to Tk1-FEH overexpression.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In T. koksaghyz roots, minimal during summer and strongly induced in autumn, peaking in November during inulin degradation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in roots of transgenic T. koksaghyz and T. brevicorniculatum lines; expression level negatively correlated with inulin content and positively with sugar and rubber content
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH32 family fructan 1-exohydrolase with hydrolase-specific W-A/S/G-W motif; N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Inulin/fructan degradation pathway linked to precursor supply for isoprenoid and natural rubber biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal degradation step releasing soluble sugars from stored inulin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Inulin (preferred, DP > 4); also weak activity on nystose and 1-kestose
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fructose and sucrose released from inulin breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes terminal fructosyl residues from inulin/fructan polymers to release fructose and sucrose; does not hydrolyze sucrose as sole substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for mobilizing inulin reserves toward sugar supply; supported but not formally proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole/tonoplast-associated secretory pathway inferred from acidic pH optimum and signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural rubber is an industrial raw material; triterpenes/sterols are isoprenoid end products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Natural rubber in root laticifers; inulin in root parenchymal vacuoles adjacent to phloem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression degrades inulin, raises fructose and sucrose, and nearly doubles root rubber content with increased triterpenes and no fitness penalty
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tk1-FEH encodes a functional GH32 fructan 1-exohydrolase that degrades inulin but not sucrose. 2. Its native root expression is low in summer and high in autumn, matching seasonal inulin breakdown. 3. Constitutive overexpression in T. koksaghyz and T. brevicorniculatum reduced inulin amount and DP, increased fructose/sucrose, elevated triterpenes, and boosted natural rubber up to ~80 mg/g DW in T. koksaghyz. 4. No detectable tradeoff in biomass, flowering, seed set, or germination was observed.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seasonal long-day to short-day transition; greenhouse growth; autumn induction; heterologous enzyme assays at pH 5.1 and 30°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seasonal qPCR-metabolite correlation; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; enzyme assays with substrate specificity and sucrose inhibition; transgenic overexpression in two dandelion species; HPLC, 1H-NMR and GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No impact on biomass, flowering, seed setting or germination reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target and marker for rubber-enriched dandelion breeding; expression level can tune optimal harvest timing and rubber yield
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sucrose dose-dependently inhibited Tk1-FEH activity; overexpression did not significantly alter 1-SST or 1-FFT transcript levels.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not functionally dissected separately; endogenous ortholog identified from roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured with conserved primers as endogenous 1-FEH background in T. brevicorniculatum roots
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ortholog identified as part of the inulin-to-sugar supply system relevant to rubber accumulation, but not directly manipulated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tb1-FEH was cloned and shown by sequence to be a close Tk1-FEH ortholog. 2. It supports the presence of conserved inulin degradation machinery in T. brevicorniculatum. 3. The experimental engineering work used Tk1-FEH rather than Tb1-FEH.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential native editing target in T. brevicorniculatum
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the principal experimentally tested enzyme in functional assays.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type seasonal roots; transgenic lines background
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression rises from spring to summer and declines to nearly undetectable by November
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seasonally expressed in roots; unchanged by Tk1-FEH overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH32 fructosyltransferase with fructosyltransferase-specific motif x-A/G-Y/F
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a fructosyl moiety from one sucrose to another to initiate inulin-type fructan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by storing carbon in inulin rather than leaving free sugars available
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for seasonal inulin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root parenchymal cell vacuoles near the phloem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes summer inulin accumulation and higher degree of polymerization; lower expression accompanies autumn sugar release
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tk1-SST was identified as a GH32 fructosyltransferase ortholog. 2. Seasonal root expression positively tracked inulin accumulation. 3. Its expression declined in autumn when inulin was degraded. 4. Tk1-FEH overexpression did not significantly alter Tk1-SST transcript levels.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growing season progression; oversupply of photoassimilates in summer; long-day to short-day seasonal change
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning, sequence motif analysis, seasonal qPCR-metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential inverse target if reduced inulin storage is desired to channel carbon toward rubber
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from orthology and expression correlation; no direct enzyme assay reported here.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type seasonal roots; transgenic lines background
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression rises during summer and remains low by autumn
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seasonally expressed in roots; unchanged by Tk1-FEH overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH32 fructosyltransferase with fructosyltransferase-specific motif x-A/G-Y/F
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Existing fructan/inulin oligomers and sucrose-derived fructosyl donors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fructosyl units between fructan molecules to elongate inulin chains and raise DP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by sequestering carbon in storage fructan
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key contributor to inulin DP and accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root parenchymal cell vacuoles near the phloem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seasonal increase in inulin amount and degree of polymerization; lower expression accompanies autumn degradation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tk1-FFT was identified as a GH32 fructosyltransferase ortholog. 2. Seasonal expression matched increasing inulin content and DP during summer. 3. Expression declined in autumn during inulin breakdown. 4. Tk1-FEH overexpression did not significantly change Tk1-FFT transcript levels.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growing season progression; summer photoassimilate surplus; autumn sink-to-source transition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning, sequence motif analysis, seasonal qPCR-metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to lower inulin polymerization and redirect carbon toward rubber
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from orthology and expression correlation; no direct enzyme assay reported here.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Tb1.2; Tb4.2; Tk1.7; Tk2.8; Tk8.1; controls Tb-WT; Tk3.1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter used for Tk1-FEH overexpression only
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; endogenous CPT increased in some high-rubber Tk1-FEH lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots of 18-week-old transgenic lines; significantly elevated in Tb1.2, Tk2.8 and Tk8.1; comparable in 40-week-old T. brevicorniculatum lines
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Isopentenyl diphosphate and allylic diphosphate primer(s)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-1,4-isoprene chain elongation within the rubber transferase complex
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control component of the rubber transferase complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher endogenous CPT expression correlated with elevated rubber accumulation in Tk1-FEH overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPT transcript levels positively correlated with natural rubber accumulation in Tk1-FEH-overexpressing plants. 2. CPT encodes a rubber transferase complex enzyme required for poly(cis-1,4-isoprene) biosynthesis. 3. Sugar mobilization from inulin breakdown is associated with increased CPT expression in high-rubber lines.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident fitness penalty in high-rubber lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong co-target/marker for rubber metabolic engineering together with FEH, RTA and REF
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPT was not directly transformed in this study, but it is a core pathway gene mechanistically linked to the rubber phenotype.
